front: Work: three men swear an oath in a peach garden to become sworn brothers during the Yellow Turban Rebellion

front: Work: characters who met at a tavern bring a black bull and white horse to a garden and vow to die 'on the same day, in the same month, in the same year'

front: Work: a leader visits the thatched cottage of a man nicknamed 'Sleeping Dragon' three times to recruit him

front: Work: a strategist captures and releases a barbarian leader seven times until he pledges allegiance

front: Work: a character creates a wooden sculpture of himself that causes his rival to flee in fear after his death

front: Work: a character lays a Stone Sentinel Maze to discourage pursuit; his father-in-law leads a man out of it

front: Work: a character claims to enjoy killing in his sleep and kills a servant putting a blanket on him to prove it
back: Romance of the Three Kingdoms (Luo Guanzhong)

front: Work: a character sets 'chicken ribs' as a password and is enraged when a subordinate correctly interprets its meaning
back: Romance of the Three Kingdoms (Luo Guanzhong)

front: Work: a character slaughters a house full of people after mistaking plans to cook a pig as plans to kill him
back: Romance of the Three Kingdoms (Luo Guanzhong)

front: Work: one character tells another they are the only two real heroes, causing the other to pretend to be scared by lightning

front: Work: a character tosses his infant son on the ground after one of his generals risked his life to save the baby
back: Romance of the Three Kingdoms (Luo Guanzhong)

front: Work: a character claims to enjoy his new life too much to think about his former home
back: Romance of the Three Kingdoms (Luo Guanzhong)

front: Work: a hunter claims to serve wolf's meat, but the guest discovers it was the flesh of the hunter's wife
back: Romance of the Three Kingdoms (Luo Guanzhong)

front: Work: one general defeats 6 other generals in order to pass through 5 passes
back: Romance of the Three Kingdoms (Luo Guanzhong)

front: Work: one character receives an empty food box from his superior, interpreting it as an order to kill himself

back: Romance of the Three Kingdoms (Luo Guanzhong)

front: Work: the protagonist of Lu Xun's 'Storm in a Teacup' is obsessed with rereading this classic Chinese novel
